categories.remilia.example.title: Remilia Ex.
categories.remilia.example.description: Examples of Remilia functions. This category is only available in a development environment.
pages.remilia.remilia.static.title: Static Macros
pages.remilia.remilia.static.text: This is an example of a macro originating from Remilia which never changes: 'exampleblue'.$(br2)$(exampleblue)This is it in effect.$()
pages.remilia.remilia.dynamic.title: Dynamic Macros
pages.remilia.remilia.dynamic.text: This entry has two macros from Remilia. They are injected the normal way, but through the code they change. Here, 'rainbowClient' changes every client tick while 'discoServer' changes every twenty server ticks. Patchouli refreshes text for regular macros when you re-open the book.$(br)$(rainbowClient)rainbowClient.$() $(discoServer)discoServer.$()
pages.remilia.remilia.methodcalling.text: Another pretty useful feature -- method-calling macros. Method-calling macros are defined in the 'method_calling_macros' key of your modid/remilia/book.json. They work like normal macros, except they call a method (given in the macro key's value) to define their value and give a string (delimited by '^(your macro here)', or the rest of the page) for the argument.
pages.remilia.remilia.methodcalling.exam: Remilia adds the 'blue_red_gradient' as a method-calling macro. Here it is in action:$(br2)$(blue_red_gradient)This text phases from blue to red.^$(blue_red_gradient)
